title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, was arrested in Albany, NY by an FBI SWAT team for two armed bank robberies.
Sawyer allegedly robbed banks in Nashua, NH and Boonton, NJ, taking $6,000 and $12,000, respectively.
The arrest was part of Operation Summer Heat 2.0, with multiple agencies involved, including FBI Boston and local police.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, was found dead near his motorcycle off Route 2 in Shelburne, NH, after going missing July 4.
A resident searching for Grondin located his body and motorcycle on Route 2 around 10:10 p.m. Thursday, police said.
Preliminary investigation suggests Grondin crossed lanes, struck a post, and crashed; no other vehicle is believed involved.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11, 2026, as 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts.
Kahan is recognized for his musical contributions and mental health advocacy in New England.
He made history with four sold-out shows at Boston's Fenway Park, attracting over 150,000 fans.

title: ‘Let’s just call it a tour’: Judy Collins says her farewell tour isn’t a farewell
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Judy Collins continues her 'farewell tour' through March 2027, denying it's an actual farewell.
The tour will be filmed for a documentary on Collins' life, music, and legacy.
Collins' New England performances include stops in Cape Cod, Cohasset, and Tanglewood with special guests.

title: 3 new movies to watch on Netflix in July
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'A League of Their Own' (1992) features Geena Davis in a 1940s women's pro baseball team story on Netflix.
'Moneyball' (2011) starring Brad Pitt as Oakland Athletics GM Billy Beane is now available on Netflix.
'Gone Girl' (2014) with Ben Affleck as Nick Dunne, whose wife Amy goes missing, joins Netflix's July lineup.

title: Red Sox win 7th straight game just hours after landing in New York
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox won their seventh consecutive game, defeating the New York Mets 6-2 at Citi Field.
Sonny Gray pitched six innings, allowing one run and three strikeouts, securing his 11th win this season.
Anthony Seigler's two-run homer fueled the Red Sox offense; he has a .292 average in 20 games since joining.

title: Christian Gonzalez responds to report that he isn’t physical enough for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots cornerback Christian Gonzalez criticized Greg Bedard on social media for questioning his physicality.
Some within the Patriots reportedly view Gonzalez as not physical enough, despite strong performance and upcoming contract extension.
Gonzalez led a Patriots secondary now bolstered by new additions like safety Kevin Byard and cornerback Carlton Davis.

title: Here’s who experts have the Red Sox selecting with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Athletic predicts the Red Sox will draft Chase Brunson, a 21-year-old outfielder from TCU, at No. 20.
Bleacher Report suggests Boston will choose Taylor Rabe, a right-handed pitcher from Ole Miss, at the 2026 MLB Draft.
CBS Sports forecasts the selection of Bo Lowrance, a high school third baseman, as Boston's first-round pick.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Long Walk,' a Stephen King adaptation, is now streaming on HBO Max featuring a deadly race in a dystopian America.
Ryan Gosling stars in 'Project Hail Mary,' a sci-fi adventure on Prime Video about a teacher in space as humanity's last hope.
Jennifer Garner's 'The Five-Star Weekend,' filmed on Nantucket, debuts on Peacock as a series about friendship and coping with loss.

title: A 4th Red Sox player has been named a 2026 MLB All-Star
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ceddanne Rafaela replaces injured Aaron Judge as a 2026 MLB All-Star, marking his first selection since debuting in 2023.
Rafaela joins Red Sox's Willson Contreras, Ranger Suarez, and Aroldis Chapman on the American League All-Star team.
Rafaela, a consistent performer, is batting .283 with eight homers and has made just one error in 734.1 innings this season.
